entries.title: Natural Raises $30 Million to Build Payment Rails for AI Agents
entries.summary: Natural, a year-old company that is building a foundational payment stack for artificial intelligence agents, raised $30 million in a Series A funding round. The round brings Natural’s total funding to more than $40 million, the company said in a Monday (July 20) press release. The firm announced about 10 months ago, in September 2025, […] The post Natural Raises $30 Million to Build Payment

entries.title: Hugging Face Latest Company Dealing With AI Cyberattacks
entries.summary: Hugging Face, whose platform hosts AI datasets, has reportedly suffered an AI-powered data breach. As TechCrunch reported Monday (July 20), the company revealed the breach last week but said it was still determining if any customer or partner data had been stolen. On its blog, Hugging Face said a dataset uploaded to its platform exploited a security vulnerability […] The post Hugging Face La

entries.title: The Impact of AI and Other New Technologies on Payment Hubs
entries.summary: Joining the FinextraTV studio at EBADay 2026 in Copenhagen, Peter Reynolds, CEO, Otoma to share his insights on AI and vendor dependence. Reynolds began by questioning whether banks were using AI in the best areas, suggesting there could be areas such as forecasting, infrastructure support and other applications that are not being fully utilised - he goes as far as to suggest a future where a new

entries.title: HSBC joins EPAA agentic AI working group as founding member
entries.summary: The Emerging Payments Association Asia (EPAA) has launched the AI & Agentic Payments Working Group with founding member HSBC, bringing together the banks, payment networks, fintechs and technology platforms that will define the standards to make agentic commerce work safely and at scale across Asia Pacific (APAC).

entries.title: Trump Orders Defense Contractors to End Reliance on Suppliers From Adversary Nations
entries.summary: President Donald Trump signed an executive order Monday (July 20) that requires defense contractors to map their supply chains and encourages them to shift their sourcing of critical materials to suppliers in the United States or partner nations. The order aims to secure the critical materials and components needed to manufacture cutting-edge equipment used on modern battlefields, the White House

entries.title: Independent Prediction Markets Converged on Nearly Identical World Cup Probabilities as Trading Hit Record Highs
entries.summary: Despite operating separate order books, liquidity pools and user bases, Polymarket and Kalshi priced Spain’s chances of winning the World Cup at almost exactly the same probability ahead of Sunday’s final.
